articles.title: Nestle to cut artificial colourings from all products

articles.title: France records 300 excess deaths during unseasonal May heatwave
articles.description: A five-day heatwave in May caused at least 300 more deaths than expected across France, health officials said Tuesday. The unseasonal heatwave was followed in June by 11 days of the hottest temperatures the country has experienced since records began.

articles.title: NBI-7 uncovers two alleged ghost flood control projects in Balamban
articles.description: CEBU CITY, Philippines — The National Bureau of Investigation–Central Visayas Regional Office (NBI-7) has uncovered two alleged ghost flood control projects in Barangay Singsing, Balamban, Cebu. NBI-7 Supervising Agent Gregorio Y. Algoso Jr. announced the discovery during a press conference on June 30, 2026. Algoso said the investigation began in December 2025 after authorities received reports of irregularities involving two flood control projects in the municipality. READ: NBI -7 on flood control projects in Cebu: Signs of substandard ones seen According to Algoso, investigators from the NBI-7’s Mandaue District Office found that the coordinates indicated in the contract for one [...]... articles.title: Lubang imposes ‘no sail policy’ for motorized bancas amid rough sea
articles.description: CALAPAN CITY, Oriental Mindoro — The municipal government of Lubang in Occidental Mindoro has imposed a “no sail policy” for all motorized bancas starting July 1, citing rough sea brought about by a low pressure area. In Memorandum No. 013, Series of 2026, all motorized bancas are prohibited from venturing into the sea until further notice to ensure the safety of fisherfolk, sea travelers and boat operators. The order was signed by Mayor Michael L. Orayani, who also chairs the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (MDRRMC). The memorandum, dated June 30, said the prevailing weather disturbance has caused [...]... Keep on reading: Lubang imposes ‘no sail policy’ for motorized bancas amid rough sea

articles.title: Houseplant hacks: will a temperature drop make my orchid bloom?
articles.description: Got a stick in a pot that you’re tempted to bin? All it needs is this little-known signal to flower again ... The problem Most of us have bought an orchid, enjoyed its flowers, then been left with a couple of leaves and a bare spike. Many assume the show is over and bin it or leave it on the sill out of guilt, watering it occasionally while expecting nothing. There it sits, dormant, waiting for a signal most people never think to give. The hack Phalaenopsis orchids rebloom in response to a temperature drop. In their natural habitat, a cooler spell signals the change of season and triggers the plant to produce a new flower spike. Recreating that shift is the prompt most orchids are waiting for, and it’s simpler to do than you might think. Continue reading...

articles.title: Venezuela earthquakes: Deaths top 1,700; US reopens port for aid
articles.description: LA GUAIRA, Venezuela – The US military reopened a major port in Venezuela to ease aid deliveries on Monday as the death toll from last week’s devastating twin earthquakes surpassed 1,700 with tens of thousands still reported missing. Five days after powerful back-to-back quakes flattened entire neighborhoods in the coastal state of La Guaira, the task of recovering the dead loomed large, as hopes of finding survivors faded. At a makeshift morgue set up in a port warehouse in La Guaira, hundreds of bodies were stored in white and black body bags and coffins waiting to be identified, an AFP correspondent saw. READ: [...]... Keep on reading: Venezuela earthquakes: Deaths top 1,700; US reopens port for aid

articles.title: Horoscope for Tuesday, June 30, 2026
articles.description:
Moon Alert

There are no restrictions to shopping or important decisions today. The moon is in Capricorn.

Aries (March 21-April 19)

A positive day

Today might begin in a stressful way, especially with parents, bosses, VIPs and the police, but this does not have to be the tone of your whole day. Starting today, the next 12 months offer opportunities for romance, vacations and joy with kids. Yes!

Taurus (April 20-May 20)

An average day

Steer clear of controversial issues because they will be a bummer earlier in the morning. Be reassured that starting today and for the next 12 months, lucky Jupiter will help you improve your home or find a better one. Family relations will be happier!

Gemini (May 21-June 20)

A positive day

Even though you might be concerned about a financial squeeze this morning — perhaps it's hard to pay bills? (Join the club; we number millions.) Fortunately, today lucky Jupiter enters a part of your chart that will boost your optimism for the next 12 months. Happy days ahead!

Cancer (June 21-July 22)

An average day

There's good news and there's bad news. The bad news (but not entirely) is that Mercury retrograde is taking place in your sign. The good news (entirely!) is lucky moneybags Jupiter will make you richer in the next 12 months. We like!

Leo (July 23-Aug. 22)

A positive day

Great news! Big-hearted, generous, moneybags Jupiter moves into glamorous, loyal Leo today to stay for a year. This will make your life more fortunate. It will bolster your optimism. It will attract favorable circumstances to you. Go for Baroque!

Virgo (Aug. 23-Sept. 22)

An average day

Today begins with some challenges with your kids or a romantic partner. Someone sucked the joy out of your morning. However, as the day wears on, you feel lighter and brighter. In fact, the next 12 months will be meaningful in a spiritual way for you.

Libra (Sept. 23-Oct. 22)

A positive day

Lucky Jupiter is moving into the part of your chart that will activate your interaction with friends, clubs and organizations for the next 12 months. Friends will benefit you. Whatever you put out will come back to you. A popular window ahead!

Scorpio (Oct. 23-Nov. 21)

An average day

"Look, Ma! No hands!" Today, lucky Jupiter moves to the top of your chart for the first time in 12 years. It will stay there for one year, boosting your profession, career, social status, and reputation in the community. This is your chance to move ahead through a promotion or public recognition. Yay!

Sagittarius (Nov. 22-Dec. 21)

A positive day

Today, lucky Jupiter moves into your fellow fire sign for 12 months. This is a big help! It's like adding the third leg to a three-legged stool. In the coming year, you'll be able to sit back, enjoy life and feel great. Finances look good as well. You wanted more?

Capricorn (Dec. 22-Jan. 19)

An average day

You like financial security because you like well-planned endings. This is why you'll be pleased to know that moneybags Jupiter is moving into one of your money houses today to stay for 12 months, which will boost your wealth indirectly through a partner or third party. It will attract money to you.

Aquarius (Jan. 20-Feb. 18)

An average day

Today lucky Jupiter is moving opposite your sign into your house of partnerships, making the next 12 months the best time for Aquarians to get married in over a decade. (You've got 11 months to meet someone special.) Seriously, this improves all your closest relationships. Sweet!

Pisces (Feb. 19-March 20)

An average day

Starting today and for the next 12 months, you'll have wonderful opportunities to improve your health as well as your job. If you can't improve your job, you'll find a better one. You will also experience greater joy with pets.

If today is your birthday

Actress Lizzy Caplan (1982) shares your birthday today. You are sensitive to others. You're also optimistic, imaginative and practical. This is a year of new beginnings, adventures and major changes in your life. Keep your eyes open for new opportunities. Define your goals. What you do now will bring great benefits in the future.

articles.title: How MSF is responding to the earthquakes in Venezuela
articles.description: Country: Venezuela (Bolivarian Republic of) Source: Médecins Sans Frontières What is the situation in Venezuela after the earthquakes? On 24 June 2026, two powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela one after another. They have caused widespread damage, with the cities of La Guaira and Caracas particularly affected. At least 1,430 people have died and 3,230 have been injured, according to the authorities. 1 Search and rescue operations are still ongoing, even as the initial emergency trauma phase is coming to an end. Hospitals that were overwhelmed during the first days after the earthquakes are now receiving fewer patients. It is estimated that over 1,500 buildings were damaged, with most of them being residential. This has displaced many families, who are now in need of shelter, water and sanitation services, food, and medical care. How is MSF responding to the earthquakes in Venezuela? During the first hours after the earthquakes, MSF was able to donate trauma kits to hospitals in La Guaira and Caracas. These kits filled supply gaps after hospitals had exhausted their own stocks treating the injured. The kits, which we continue to donate, are made to prevent rapid blood loss from traumatic injuries . So far, eight hospitals across the two cities have received donations from us, covering the needs of approximately 3,500 patients. Now that the initial trauma phase is coming to an end, we are turning our focus to the informal displacement sites that people are staying in. We plan to provide medical care via mobile clinics, and provide mental health care . What is MSF seeing in Caracas and La Guaira? Our colleagues report that some hospitals needed multiple donations of materials during the initial trauma phase of the emergency. People will likely need psychological support to cope with the devastation of the earthquakes, so we are looking into the mental health needs in different parks and stadiums where people are staying in Caracas.

articles.title: Trump Melts Down About ‘SCUM’ as Green Slime Takes Over Reflecting Pool
articles.description: Photo Illustration by Thomas Levinson/The Daily Beast/Getty/Reuters President Donald Trump has blasted “SCUM” for the green slime which has engulfed the Reflecting Pool—although not the single-cellular type rapidly spreading across it. The 47th president went on a social media ramble about the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, in which he vowed it would be restored to perfect condition while blasting “Radical Left SCUM” who he blames for its state. Trump, 80, has obsessed about the Reflecting Pool ever since he bragged that he would be the first president to permanently fix it since it was originally built in the 1920s. But his efforts to date have come up dramatically short while his rants have only drawn further attention to the botched project. Read more at The Daily Beast.
